title: [BUG] Default font size breaks column widths
author: lket
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

<!-- A clear and concise description of what the bug is. -->

Lib version: 4.3.0 (and many older versions, possibly from 1.14.0)

Issue #816 looks related, but the fix doesn't seem to work in the current version.

## Steps To Reproduce

<!-- The exact steps required to reproduce the issue, ideally with a code example -->

Start with a xlsx file that has as default body font something other than Calibri 11. For example, latest desktop Excel on Windows 10 uses Calibri 12 as the default font, which causes this issue. The issue is most clear with very large font size. I have attached an example that uses body font Calibri 72.

[test.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/7250252/test.xlsx)

```javascript
const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
await workbook.xlsx.readFile("test.xlsx");
await workbook.xlsx.writeFile("output.xlsx");
```

Compare the input and output. Column widths (and other details in the interface) are different. This can cause surprising problems, as the default font is not usually easily visible in the interface.

Why default font affects column widths: <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office/troubleshoot/excel/determine-column-widths>

## The expected behaviour:

The input and output are identical.


## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

My guess is that this is related to how the default font is set in [lib\xlsx\xform\style\styles-xform.js:240 (addStyleModel)](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/master/lib/xlsx/xform/style/styles-xform.js#L240). It looks like maybe the default font is inserted *before* the styles have been read from the input file. In the normal case the default font should not be inserted, because one already exists in the input.

Thoughts: Similar check is already done in render, is this earlier one necessary at all? If it is, could it be done at the end of  addStyleModel, instead of in the beginning?

I'm also experiencing this issue.
It would be great if the styles of a loaded worksheet would be retained.
